# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: internal-server
  namespace: my-service
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-service
  port: 8080
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/2

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: allow-public
  namespace: my-service
spec:
  server:
    name: internal-server
  client:
    unauthenticated: true

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: internal-server-secure
  namespace: secure-service
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: secure-service
  port: 443
  proxyProtocol: HTTPS

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: allow-internal
  namespace: secure-service
spec:
  server:
    name: internal-server-secure
  client:
    serviceAccount:
      name: internal-service
      namespace: default
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: grpc-server
  namespace: grpc-service
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: grpc-service
  port: 5001
  proxyProtocol: gRPC

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: allow-grpc
  namespace: grpc-service
spec:
  server:
    name: grpc-server
  client:
    serviceAccount:
      name: grpc-client
      namespace: default
